arguments.task: Research Vercel using official first-party sources where possible. Open https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api and extract: one-line product description; whether the REST API is public and what authentication methods are documented; any docs about access tokens, OAuth, or bearer tokens; any pricing or access gating pages indicating self-serve or paid requirements for API use; any rate limit documentation; and whether an official MCP server exists on Vercel docs or GitHub. Also gather exact supporting URLs from docs, pricing, changelog, GitHub, or support pages. Return a concise structured summary with claims mapped to source URLs. If needed, navigate to linked official pages such as authentication docs, rate limits docs, pricing docs, and any official MCP page.

input.thought: Start a browser research task on official Vercel docs and linked first-party pages.

arguments.task: Continue the Vercel research in this browser session. Using official Vercel docs only if possible, find exact URLs and quotes for: 1) whether Vercel integrations use OAuth 2.0 and where that is documented; 2) whether using the Vercel REST API requires a paid plan or is available self-serve to any account; look for pricing or limits docs that indicate plan availability; 3) exact rate-limit values from official docs; 4) exact one-line description of the Vercel REST API from the main docs; 5) official Vercel MCP documentation URL and whether it says available on all plans. Return a concise structured summary with each claim paired to its URL.

input.thought: Launch a narrower follow-up task in the same session to resolve OAuth and access-tier ambiguities.

raw_result.description: Frontend cloud platform whose REST API lets you interact with Vercel projects and deploy applications programmatically.
raw_result.auth_notes: The REST API docs include an Authentication section documenting Bearer authentication with Vercel Access Tokens. Vercel integrations documentation appears to indicate OAuth 2.0 for third-party integrations, but the exact OAuth page URL was not cleanly captured in the gathered evidence, so OAuth support should be human-verified from the integrations docs.
raw_result.gating_reason: Evidence gathered from official docs indicates the REST API is publicly documented and usable with account-level access tokens; no contact-sales or admin approval gate was found. Rate-limit docs show plan-based limits (for example Hobby vs Pro), implying self-serve access with higher limits on paid plans rather than API availability being gated.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API docs exist for interacting with Vercel projects and deployments; official docs also expose related integrations and MCP documentation, suggesting a meaningful surface for deployment, project, and platform operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Vercel docs include a Vercel MCP server page and the browser run reported it as available on all plans, but the exact wording should be rechecked by a human from the cited docs page.

raw_result.blocker: Core REST API appears buildable today, but some important implementation details remain insufficiently verified from the captured evidence set, especially exact OAuth/integrations auth documentation and precise access-tier wording for free vs paid usage.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits documentation includes REST API rate limits with plan-based differences (the browser evidence explicitly noted Hobby vs Pro distinctions), but exact numeric thresholds were not reliably extracted into chat and should be human-verified on the limits page before publication.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official first-party REST API docs are public; authentication uses Bearer access tokens; official integrations docs suggest OAuth support for app integrations; and Vercel publishes official MCP documentation, which is a strong signal for agent/tooling friendliness.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The existing evidence supports public REST API docs, bearer-token auth, and official MCP existence, but exact OAuth documentation, precise rate-limit values, and definitive self-serve free-tier wording were not fully captured in-chat and should be verified directly from the cited Vercel pages.
